mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate equity risk premium expansion and negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or cross-sectional IC remains above 0.05 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in real GDP and new housing starts confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with concurrent GDPC1 and HOUST growth, or interaction term coefficient fails to exceed 0.04 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Within a post-shock recovery regime, federal funds rates remaining below 1.0 percent signal sustained accommodative liquidity that compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive 20-day forward market returns.
failure_condition: Equity risk premiums fail to compress when rates stay below 1%, or forward returns are flat or negative despite the trigger being active for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2.0 percent indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing order books, which reduces near-term earnings guidance and produces negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO drops above the 2.0 percent threshold, or rolling Spearman correlation exceeds -0.1 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly employment growth exceeding 100,000 jobs and consumer price inflation rising above 0.3 percent confirms a healthy wage-price dynamic without overheating, which fuels short-term equity momentum and positive 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Short-term momentum fails to materialize when both series rise simultaneously, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05) during joint trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail and food services sales revenues reflects expanding consumer spending power, which directly lifts trailing and forward earnings multiples and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not track RSAFS growth trends, or predictive R-squared falls below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, monthly federal funds rate increases exceeding 0.25 percentage points raise corporate borrowing costs and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are not negative during rapid hike episodes, or valuation compression fails to materialize as evidenced by flat or rising forward P/E ratios.
